subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Role Cards (16 Total)
- **1** Doppelganger
- **2** Werewolves
- **1** Minion
- **2** Masons
- **1** Seer
- **1** Robber
- **1** Troublemaker
- **1** Drunk
- **1** Insomniac
- **1** Hunter
- **1** Tanner
- **3** Villagers

#### Tokens
- **16** role tokens

#### Player-Count Reference List (Step 0 Audit)
- **Supported player count** — 3-10 players.
- **Role-set formula** — Always use player count + 3 role cards.
- **Center cards** — Always place exactly 3 face-down cards in the center.
- **Center tokens** — Place one role token for every role card used in the session.
- **No other count-specific rule changes** — The provided rule text does not list unique setup/action/scoring exceptions by exact count beyond 3-10 support and card-count formula.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
--- 3-10 Players ---
- **Role selection rule** — Build the role set with exactly 3 more cards than players.
- **Token display** — Put one matching role token in the center for each role card in this game.
- **Deal rule** — Deal each player 1 face-down role card.
- **Center setup** — Place the remaining 3 role cards face down in the center.
- **Private knowledge** — Each player secretly looks at only their own card.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Turn system** — There is no normal first-player turn loop; play is phase-driven.
- **Start trigger** — Begin Night phase after all players have viewed their own role.
- **Night scope** — All role swaps/role information actions happen at night.
- **Day scope** — Discussion, claims, and accusations happen during day.
- **Elimination scope** — One simultaneous vote determines deaths.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Night phase** — Resolve wake order exactly: #1 Doppelganger, #2 Werewolves, #3 Minion, #4 Masons, #5 Seer, #6 Robber, #7 Troublemaker, #8 Drunk, #9 Insomniac, #9a Doppelganger/Insomniac check.
2. **No-wake roles** — Villager, Tanner, and Hunter never wake at night.
3. **Day phase** — All players discuss, accuse, and make claims.
4. **Vote phase** — Everyone points at another player simultaneously.
5. **Death resolution** — Highest votes die; ties kill all tied; if everyone gets one vote, no one dies.
6. **Win check** — Determine winners from final roles and death outcomes.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Night Role Actions
- **Doppelganger view** — Look at one other player's card without swapping.
- **Doppelganger identity** — Become that role and that team immediately.
- **Doppelganger immediate copy** — If you viewed Seer/Robber/Troublemaker/Drunk, do that action immediately and do not wake again for that role.
- **Doppelganger delayed wake** — If you viewed Werewolf or Mason, wake with that role later when called.
- **Doppelganger/Minion case** — If you viewed Minion, you are on werewolf team and follow the Minion interaction.
- **Doppelganger/Insomniac case** — If you viewed Insomniac, wake at #9a to check your card.

- **Werewolf action** — Werewolves open eyes and identify each other.
- **Lone-wolf option** — If you are the only werewolf player, you may view 1 center card.

- **Minion action** — Minion wakes after werewolves and sees who the werewolves are (werewolves show thumbs up).
- **Minion visibility** — Werewolves do not learn who the Minion is.

- **Mason action** — If Masons are in game, both must be included; each Mason wakes and checks for the other Mason.
- **Mason center clue** — If a Mason sees no other Mason awake, the other Mason card is in center.

- **Seer action** — Look at 1 player's card OR 2 center cards; do not move cards.

- **Robber action** — You may swap your card with another player's card, then look at your new card.
- **Robber team rule** — You are on the team of the role you took.
- **Robber limit** — You do not perform your new role's night action.
- **Robber pass option** — If you do not rob, you remain Robber on village team.

- **Troublemaker action** — You may swap cards of 2 other players without looking at them.

- **Drunk action** — You must swap your Drunk card with 1 center card and do not look at it.

- **Insomniac action** — Wake late and look at your own card to see whether it changed.

#### Day Actions
- **Discussion action** — Share or hide night information, bluff, and challenge claims.
- **Vote action** — Point at exactly one player during simultaneous vote.

#### No-Wake Roles
- **Villager** — No night action; village team.
- **Tanner** — No night action; independent win condition.
- **Hunter** — No night action; adds extra death if Hunter dies.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Final Role Logic
- **Role drift** — Starting role can differ from final role due to swaps/copies.
- **Resolution basis** — Most team checks use final role state after all night actions.

#### Team Exceptions
- **Robber exception** — Robber changes team to stolen role but does not trigger that role's night action.
- **Minion alignment** — Minion is on werewolf team even though werewolves may not know who Minion is.

#### Independent Objective
- **Tanner objective** — Tanner wins only by dying.
- **Cross-team overlap** — Tanner can win in outcomes where other teams also win.

#### Chain Death Rule
- **Hunter trigger** — If Hunter dies, the player Hunter points at also dies regardless of vote count.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **Scoring model** — There is no VP track or point tally.
- **End trigger** — The game ends after the single vote resolves all deaths (including Hunter chain death).

#### Win Conditions
- **Village win** — At least one Werewolf dies.
- **Werewolf-team win** — No Werewolves die.
- **No-werewolf special case** — If all werewolf cards are in center, village wins only if no one dies.
- **Tanner win** — Tanner wins only if Tanner dies.

#### Interaction Clarifications
- **Tanner blocks werewolf win** — If Tanner dies and no Werewolf dies, werewolf team does not win.
- **Dual result case** — If Tanner and a Werewolf both die, village still wins and Tanner also wins.

#### Vote Outcome Rules
- **Most-votes rule** — Player with most votes dies.
- **Tie-votes rule** — All tied players die.
- **All-single-vote rule** — If every player receives exactly one vote, no one dies.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Timeline first** — Open with night-order details (what you saw, when, and whether swaps happened).
- **Cross-check early** — If your claim can be verified (Mason pair, Robber target, Insomniac check), state names early.
- **Information role discipline** — As Seer/Robber/Insomniac, decide quickly whether immediate reveal creates stronger vote control than delayed reveal.
- **Consistency pressure** — Ask players to restate their role claim in wake-order terms to expose contradictions.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Static-role thinking** — Treating starting roles as final roles leads to bad vote targets.
- **No-werewolf blind spot** — Ignoring the all-center-werewolf possibility can hand village a loss by unnecessary kill.
- **Unverified trust** — Accepting lone claims without cross-links makes coordinated lies easy.
- **Minion oversight** — Forgetting Minion incentives causes misreads of anti-village behavior.
- **Hunter mismanagement** — Piling votes on Hunter without planning pointer consequences causes avoidable double deaths.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Reveal timing** — Reveal hard info early to steer votes, or hold it to trap liars after they commit.
- **Vote risk profile** — Choose between high-confidence werewolf shot and conservative no-kill logic in possible all-center-werewolf games.
- **Claim posture** — Decide whether to hard-claim (control narrative) or soft-claim (retain flexibility).

metadata.notes: Written from provided official rulebook text including wake order, role actions, vote resolution, and win conditions.
